beat1.headline: Music na the thing wey dey bring strangers together
beat1.subtext3: Across language wey you never talk before
beat2.text: The beat dey cross every border. The words no fit do am.
beat4.prefix: For the first time ever for commercial dance track
beat4.headline: One track. 50 languages. 5.8 billion voices.
geo.detected: E be like say you dey {{country}}.
geo.pickSubtitle: Select the language wey you like make we continue
dialect.en: Melbourne English dey hit different for dancefloor - e get dat cool Aussie vibe wey mix with immigrant energy from everywhere. When locals dey shout "Get around it!" on top heavy techno, you know say the night don ready to scatter!
whyCity.en: Melbourne warehouse parties for Collingwood and Fitzroy na legendary thing - raw concrete spaces where minimal techno dey meet maximum euphoria. The city laneway culture dey enter their club scene, dey create intimate spaces where world-class DJs dey drop sets until sunrise.
dialect.zh: Shanghai Mandarin for dancefloor na pure silk and electricity - sharp sounds dey cut through bass drops while "牛逼" (niú bī) dem dey shout when the beat hit perfect. Na the sound of China future generation dey claim their night.
whyCity.zh: The Bund underground scene dey pulse under Shanghai neon skyline, where venues like Elevator and ALL Club dey host China most progressive electronic music. Na here East dey meet West for dancefloor, dey create sound wey be uniquely Shanghai.
dialect.hi: Mumbai Hindi na Bollywood vocals meet underground bass - when dancers dey scream "Kya baat hai!" on top crushing drops, the energy na pure Bombay street hustle wey translate to four-four time. Na the sound of eight million dreams wey compress into one night.
whyCity.hi: Bandra and Lower Parel club scene na where Bollywood producers dey party next to tech bros and film stars. Venues like Kitty Su dey attract international DJs wey dey remix classical Indian elements with cutting-edge electronica, dey create Mumbai signature sound.
dialect.ar-eg: Egyptian Arabic dey flow like Nile through dance music - those rolling "r"s and deep sounds dey create rhythms wey sync perfectly with tribal house beats. When the crowd dey shout "Khalas!" the night don officially peak.
whyCity.ar-eg: Cairo after midnight na ancient mysticism meet modern enjoyment. For Zamalek underground venues, traditional oud melodies dey blend with electronic beats while Nile dey shine outside, dey create otherworldly dancefloor experience wey be pure Egyptian magic.
dialect.fr: Parisian French for dancefloor na pure seduction - those nasal vowels and connection sounds dey create linguistic groove wey match deep house perfectly. When the crowd dey whisper "C'est fou!" you know say the set don transcend into art.
whyCity.fr: Paris invent house music sophisticated cousin for venues like Rex Club and Concrete. The city obsession with musical perfection mean say every beat craft like haute couture, dey create dancefloors wey feel more like sonic sculptures pass parties.
dialect.bn: Bengali liquid consonants and flowing vowels dey create natural polyrhythms wey sync beautifully with jungle and drum'n'bass. When Dhaka ravers dey shout "Darun!" the excitement dey cut through monsoon humidity and pounding 808s.
whyCity.bn: Dhaka underground scene dey thrive for Gulshan and Dhanmondi, where traditional Bengali folk rhythms dey merge with electronic beats for intimate venues. The city young energy and musical heritage dey create unique sound wey be both ancient and futuristic.
dialect.ru: Russian consonant clusters dey hit like hammer blows over hard techno - when Moscow ravers dey growl "Офигенно!" (Ofigenno!) through the bass, e dey sound like industrial machinery wey achieve consciousness and choose to dance.
whyCity.ru: Moscow club scene dey burn bright for converted Soviet spaces where history dey meet enjoyment. Venues like Arma17 and Gipsy dey host world-renowned techno DJs for brutal concrete settings wey amplify the music raw power.
dialect.ur: Urdu poetry dey meet electronic poetry when classical ghazal rhythms dey sync with modern beats. The language Persian elegance and Arabic power dey create verses wey float over bass like silk scarves for strobe light.
whyCity.ur: Karachi Defense and Clifton areas dey host underground parties where qawwali traditions dey blend with contemporary electronic music. The city creative underground dey transform traditional Pakistani sounds into dancefloor gold wey move both body and soul.
dialect.pt-br: São Paulo Portuguese na pure rhythm incarnate - those nasal vowels and rolling consonants dey sync perfectly with baile funk beats. When the crowd dey scream "Tá pegando fogo!" you know say the party don reach peak Brazilian intensity.
whyCity.pt-br: São Paulo club scene for Vila Madalena and Vila Olímpia dey create Brazil most innovative electronic music. Venues like D-Edge dey host international DJs wey dey remix samba and funk carioca into cutting-edge electronic tracks wey make the whole city dance.
dialect.id: Indonesian dey flow like gamelan through house music - those open vowels and soft consonants dey create natural melodic lines wey sync with tropical house perfectly. When Balinese ravers dey shout "Mantap jiwa!" the jungle itself dey seem to pulse.
whyCity.id: Bali beach clubs for Seminyak and underground venues for Canggu dey host world-class DJs against tropical backdrops. The island spiritual energy dey transform electronic music into transcendent experiences where ancient temple rhythms dey meet modern dancefloor ecstasy.
dialect.de: Berlin German dey hit different for 6 AM when the pills dey kick in. Dem get words like "verschlimmbessern" - making something worse while you dey try improve am, wey perfectly describe every DJ set for Sisyphos.
whyCity.de: Berghain concrete cathedral dey set the global standard for enjoyment abandon. From Watergate riverside terrace to Kit Kat Club anything-goes way, Berlin dancefloors na temples where techno born and boundaries dey die.
dialect.es-mx: Mexico City Spanish dey move like cumbia - fluid, hypnotic, unstoppable. The chilangos dey drop "güey" every third word and their slang dey flow like mezcal, with phrases like "está padrísimo" dey hit harder pass any bass drop.
whyCity.es-mx: From Roma Norte underground spots to Zona Rosa neon-lit madness, CDMX clubs dey pulse with 23 million heartbeats. Patrick Miller and Rhodesia dey blend reggaeton with pre-Hispanic rhythms wey make your soul move before your feet catch up.
dialect.ja: Tokyo Japanese for dancefloor na pure poetry - dem dey talk "kimochi ii" (feels good) like prayer, and club regulars dey whisper "yabai" (insane) when the drop hit just right. Na linguistic minimalism dey meet maximum emotion.
whyCity.ja: Shibuya im neon maze dey lead to Contact and Womb, where 2,000-watt sound system dey shake skyscrapers. Tokyo club pikin dem dey dance like robot wey dey dream to be human, dem dey create moments where technology and transcendence no get difference.
dialect.pn: Punjabi for dancefloor na pure fire - "chakde phatte" dey echo through speakers as bhangra beats dey meet trap snares. The way dem dey roll "balle balle" for dem tongue fit make anybody move, even if you stone-cold sober.
whyCity.pn: Chandigarh im Sector 26 dey come alive after midnight, where dhol dey meet EDM for basement clubs. The city youth dem dey blend traditional bhangra with global beats, dem dey create sound wey be both ancient and futuristic, pure Punjab energy unleashed.
dialect.fa: Underground Tehran Farsi na rebellion wey dem wrap with poetry. Dem dey whisper "haal mikonam" (I dey vibe) like secret code, and when person talk "ajab" (amazing), you know say the night just get dangerous for the best way possible.
whyCity.fa: Behind closed doors for northern Tehran, house music dey become resistance. Private parties for Elahieh mansions dey pulse with forbidden energy, where Persian youth dey dance like their life depend on am - because sometimes e dey do so.
dialect.sw: Nairobi Swahili dey hit like Tusker lager - smooth, strong, and e go make you want more. "Haya twende" (make we go) dey become the night anthem, while "poa" (cool) dem dey throw am like confetti when the beat drop perfect.
whyCity.sw: From Westlands rooftop spots to Kilimani underground venues, Nairobi scene dey blend Afrobeats with global house. The city 4 million dreams dem dey converge for dancefloors where Gengetone dey meet techno, dem dey create East Africa most electric nights.
dialect.ar-ma: Casablanca darija dey flow like mint tea wey dem mix with whiskey. "Zwina" (beautiful) dem dey shout am over synthesizers, and when person yell "allah" mid-drop, na pure spiritual house music transcendence wey dem wrap with Moroccan street poetry.
whyCity.ar-ma: Casa Corniche clubs dey pulse with Atlantic energy, where French house dey meet Gnawa rhythms. From Pacha to underground spots for Maarif, Morocco economic capital dey dance where Europe dey meet Africa, dem dey create magnetic North African nightlife magic.
dialect.vi: Saigon Vietnamese dey bounce like Honda Wave wey dey pass District 1 traffic. "Cực chất" (super fire) dey echo through underground venues, while "bay" (flying/high) dey take new meaning when the bass drop for those hidden basement clubs.
whyCity.vi: Ho Chi Minh City nightlife dey explode after midnight for Bui Vien neon chaos. From rooftop bars wey dey overlook Saigon River to underground techno bunkers, this city energy na pure Vietnamese coffee wey meet electronic euphoria.
dialect.yue: Hong Kong Cantonese dey cut through club smoke like neon wey dey pass rain. "Diu" dey punctuate every bass drop, while "gau dim" (sorted/perfect) dey become the highest praise when DJ absolutely destroy the crowd with perfect track selection.
whyCity.yue: Central skyscraper clubs and Lan Kwai Fong underground spots dey create vertical nightlife ecosystems. Hong Kong dancefloors dey exist for the space between East and West, where Cantopop dey meet global house for Asia most cosmopolitan playground.
dialect.tr: Istanbul Turkish dey roll off tongues like raki wey dey go down throats. "Müthiş" (amazing) dem dey scream am over Bosphorus house beats, while "çok iyi" (so good) dey become mantra when DJs dey blend Eastern melodies with Western electronic fury.
whyCity.tr: From Beyoğlu rooftop clubs to Karaköy warehouse spaces, Istanbul dey dance where continents dey collide. The city nightlife dey bridge Europe and Asia with dancefloors wey dey pulse until sunrise over the Bosphorus, dem dey create transcontinental electronic magic.
dialect.tl: Manila Tagalog dey hit different when the bass drop - locals dey throw "Grabe!" and "Sobrang ganda!" between verses. The city club crowd dey mix English so seamlessly that DJs dey switch languages mid-sentence, dem dey create this electric bilingual flow.
whyCity.tl: Poblacion neon-lit clubs dey pulse with Asia most unapologetic party energy. From rooftop bars for Makati to underground spots for Quezon City, Manila dancefloors no dey sleep - literally, some venues dey go until 8 AM for weekends.
dialect.ta: Chennai Tamil dey carry the ocean rhythm for im syllables - when dem speak am over heavy beats, e dey create natural polyrhythms wey go make your body move involuntarily. Local clubbers dey pepper their conversations with "Semma beat da!"
whyCity.ta: Phoenix MarketCity clubs dey blend classical Carnatic rhythms with modern EDM, dem dey create India most unique dancefloor fusion. Chennai IT crowd dey bring serious energy to weekend sessions wey often dey spill onto Marina Beach at sunrise.
dialect.ko: Seoul Korean dey bounce with built-in aegyo - even aggressive rap verses dey sound melodic. Hongdae clubbers dey shout "Daebak!" when the drop hit, and the language natural rhythm dey match K-pop addictive beat patterns perfectly.
whyCity.ko: Gangnam underground scene dey go way deeper than PSY ever show. Clubs like Octagon and Arena dey pump until 6 AM, where chaebol kids and street dancers dey create Korea most electric cultural collision for LED-lit dancefloors.
dialect.ha: Kano Hausa dey flow like desert wind over percussion - im tonal nature dey create instant harmonies with Afrobeats. When the crowd shout "Kai!" together, the entire club dey become one massive polyrhythmic instrument.
whyCity.ha: Northern Nigeria cultural capital dey bring Sahel rhythms to modern club culture. Kano youth dem dey blend traditional Hausa poetry with contemporary beats for venues where ancient trading route energy dey meet cutting-edge sound systems.
dialect.pcm: Lagos Pidgin na pure liquid rhythm - every sentence dey sound like say e ready to drop into beat. "E dey pepper dem!" dey become the crowd battle cry, and the language naturally dey sync with Afrobeats off-kilter timing.
whyCity.pcm: Victoria Island clubs dey pulse with Africa economic heartbeat. From Quilox to DNA Lounge, Lagos dancefloors dey born the sounds wey go dominate global charts next year - na here Afrobeats royalty dey test their hottest unreleased tracks.
dialect.it: Milanese Italian dey sound like velvet wey dey cover steel – fashion week sophistication dey meet industrial underground grit. Club crowd dey punctuate drops with breathy "Madonna!" wey dey add unexpected sensuality to even the hardest techno sets.
whyCity.it: Navigli district warehouse parties dey define European techno elegance. Milan fashion elite and underground ravers dey unite for converted factories where Armani dey meet analog synthesizers, dey create Italy most stylish subversive dancefloor culture.
dialect.th: Bangkok Thai dey wrap around beats like silk – the five tones dey create instant melodic layers over any track. Street vendors outside clubs dey use the same tonal patterns wey dey make Thai rap so hypnotically catchy.
whyCity.th: Sukhumvit rooftop scenes dey blend Buddhist spirituality with hedonistic nightlife energy. From Sky Bar luxury to RCA underground grit, Bangkok dancefloors dey pulse with Southeast Asia most intoxicating cultural contradictions.
dialect.am: Addis Ababa Amharic dey carry ancient Ge'ez rhythms wey don predate modern club culture by millennia. The consonant clusters dey create natural breakbeats, while locals dey shout "Betam!" when the energy dey peak perfectly.
whyCity.am: Africa diplomatic capital dey bring continental diversity to every dancefloor. Clubs for Bole district dey mix traditional Ethiopian jazz scales with contemporary beats, where AU delegates and local youth dey create uniquely pan-African party energy.
dialect.es-co: Medellín Spanish dey roll with reggaeton DNA wey dey embedded for every syllable. Paisas dey add "¡Qué berraquera!" to celebrate peak moments, and the accent musical lilt dey make even casual conversation sound like freestyle rap.
whyCity.es-co: El Poblado transformation from cartel territory to party paradise don create Latin America most intense nightlife energy. Clubs here dey pulse with redemption and celebration – every night dey feel like the city dey dance away its dark past.
dialect.yo: Lagos Yoruba dey speak in drums – the tonal system dey mirror talking drum patterns wey dey instantly sync with Afrobeats. When crowds dey chant "E ma binu o!" the entire club dey become extension of traditional Yoruba percussion ensembles.
whyCity.yo: Victoria Island elite venues dey channel centuries of Yoruba musical tradition into cutting-edge club culture. Here, ancient rhythms dey meet modern production for spaces where Nigeria cultural heritage dey fuel Africa hottest contemporary sounds.
dialect.es-es: Madrid Spanish dey hit different after 2 AM — the lisp dey become rhythm, and 'joder' dey punctuate every beat drop. Madrileños dey stretch vowels like dem dey mix tracks, dey turn conversations into pre-game for the real party.
whyCity.es-es: Madrid clubs no dey open until midnight and dey close at sunrise — this na where European clubbing don perfect. Kapital seven floors and Fabrik warehouse raves dey create dancefloor ecosystem wey dey demand music recorded at 4 AM energy levels.
dialect.pl: Polish consonant clusters dey create natural breakbeats — words like 'przestrzeń' dey sound like scratching vinyl. Warsaw ravers don turn their impossible grammar into percussive art form wey dey match techno industrial edge.
whyCity.pl: Warsaw underground scene don explode from Soviet-era bunkers into venues like Jasna 1 and Smolna. The city concrete brutalism and historical weight dey create dancefloors where euphoria dey feel earned, revolution dey feel possible.
dialect.es-ar: Porteño Spanish dey roll off tongues like silk — the 'sh' sound for 'yo' and 'lluvia' dey create vocal textures smoother than any synthesizer. Buenos Aires don invent the sensual whisper wey dey make lyrics feel like secrets shared at 5 AM.
whyCity.es-ar: Buenos Aires dey bleed music from tango to electronic — Niceto Club and Crobar dey host crowds wey dey dance like dem dey make love to the beat. The city Italian-influenced passion dey turn every dancefloor into emotional marathon.
dialect.my: Burmese dey flow like liquid silk between tones — each syllable dey rise and fall dey create natural melodic progressions. Myanmar language no just dey carry words, e dey carry entire emotional landscapes wey electronic music desperately need.
whyCity.my: Yangon nightlife na raw, underground, and absolutely electric — venues like Minus and Gekko dey transform colonial buildings into sweat-drenched sanctuaries. This na dance music recorded where freedom still dey taste forbidden and euphoric.
dialect.uk: Ukrainian vocals dey soar with Slavic soul — every word dey contain multisyllabic poetry wey dey turn verses into anthems. Kyiv language dey carry both pain and celebration, dey create emotional depth wey dey make dancefloors feel like cathedrals.
whyCity.uk: Kyiv clubs dey pulse with defiant energy — venues like Closer and Saxon dey transform Soviet architecture into temples of resistance. Dancing here no be escapism; na act of cultural survival wey dey give electronic music real meaning.
dialect.ar-lv: Levantine Arabic dey flow like honey mixed with rebellion — Lebanese club kids dey drop French words mid-sentence and dey stretch vowels into vocal runs. Beirut dialect na pure silk wrapped around steel, perfect for vocals wey dey seduce and ignite.
whyCity.ar-lv: Beirut dey party like the world dey end because sometimes e dey feel like na so — venues like B018 (built like bunker) and BO18 dey create dancefloors where hedonism dey meet historical trauma. Pure cathartic release through sound.
dialect.ms: Malay dey bounce with infectious rhythm — words like 'gila' (crazy) and 'syok' (awesome) basically na onomatopoeia for good vibes. KL club kids dey mix English, Chinese, and Tamil mid-sentence, dey create the world most multicultural club language.
whyCity.ms: Kuala Lumpur Changkat and TREC districts dey transform into neon-lit playgrounds where East dey meet West for the dancefloor. Venues like Zouk and Marquee dey create humid, tropical euphoria wey dey make every night feel like vacation.
dialect.zu: Zulu clicks and tongue pops dey create natural percussion — the language basically na beatboxing disguised as conversation. Durban Zulu speakers dey turn every vocal into rhythmic poetry wey dey make house music feel like ancestral calling.
whyCity.zu: Durban born gqom — dat raw, minimal electronic sound wey dey conquer global dancefloors. Townships like KwaMashu and venues across the Golden Mile dey create grinding, hypnotic rhythms wey make body move by force.
dialect.ro: Romanian dey flow with Latin romance mixed with Balkan fire — Bucharest ravers dey pepper sentences with 'fain' (cool) and 'nebun' (crazy), dey create street poetry wey perfect for vocals wey need sound both elegant and dangerous.
whyCity.ro: Bucharest Old Town dey transform into Eastern Europe wildest party district — venues like Expirat and Control Club dey pack crowds till dawn. The city post-communist hedonism dey create dancefloors where inhibition go die.
dialect.nl: Dutch consonants dey hit like kick drums — words dey end with sharp 'g' sounds wey dey slice through mix layers. Amsterdam club kids dey speak in staccato bursts between puffs, dey create natural vocal chops perfect for electronic production.
whyCity.nl: Amsterdam invent the superclub with venues like Paradiso and De School — former churches and schools wey transform into temples of techno. The city liberal spirit dey create dancefloors where anything go and everything flow.
dialect.es-cl: Chilean Spanish dey drop syllables like them hot, dey turn "para" into "pa" and "todos" into "to's". When Santiaguinos get hyped for dancefloor, their rapid-fire slang become pure percussion.
whyCity.es-cl: Santiago underground scene dey thrive for converted warehouses for Ñuñoa, where the Andean altitude make every bass drop hit harder. The city rebellious spirit, wey forge under dictatorship, dey create dancefloors wey feel like liberation.
dialect.he: Hebrew dey read right-to-left but Tel Aviv club culture dey flow for every direction. The guttural sounds and rolling Rs dey create natural breakbeats, while modern slang dey borrow from Arabic, Russian, and English mid-sentence.
whyCity.he: Tel Aviv never sleep because tomorrow no dey guaranteed. From Rothschild Boulevard to the grittier south, clubs dey stay open till sunrise and beyond, dey create 24/7 dancefloor culture wey fuel by pure intensity.
dialect.el: Greek ancient rhythms dey flow through modern club speak, where "malaka" become term of endearment between dancers. The language natural musicality make every shout feel like call-and-response.
whyCity.el: Athens clubs dey hide for Psyrri crumbling buildings and Gazi industrial spaces, where economic hardship breed legendary resilience. The dancefloor become escape where bouzouki meet techno for beautiful chaos.
dialect.sv: Swedish sing-song melody dey turn club chatter into natural harmonies. Stockholmers reserved nature dey melt away for darkness, replace by intimate whispers of "lagom" - finding perfect balance for the floor.
whyCity.sv: Stockholm club scene dey pulse through Södermalm basements and waterfront venues, where long winter nights dey create spaces for deep connection. The city tech wealth dey fund world-class sound systems for intimate settings.
dialect.sr: Serbian dey flow like liquid fire, with passionate consonant clusters wey match the intensity of Belgrade nightlife. Every word sound like e dey shouted over heavy bass, even for conversation.
whyCity.sr: Belgrade splavovi (river barges) dey turn the Danube into Europe wildest floating dancefloors. This city dey party like tomorrow no dey because history teach them say e fit be true.
dialect.ca: Catalan rolling rhythms dey hide rebellious power - to speak am for Barcelona dancefloors na act of cultural defiance. The language musicality make every chant feel like freedom song.
whyCity.ca: Barcelona club scene dey span from Gràcia intimate spaces to beachfront superclubs, where Mediterranean hedonism meet Catalonian independence spirit. The city invent the concept of dancing till noon.
dialect.pt-eu: European Portuguese dey swallow vowels and slur consonants, dey create dreamy flow perfect for late-night confessions. Lisbon fado-influenced rhythm make every club conversation sound like poetry.
whyCity.pt-eu: Lisbon nightlife dey flow through Bairro Alto cobblestone maze and Cais do Sodré converted warehouses. The city melancholic soul dey transform dancefloors into spaces of beautiful, bittersweet release.
dialect.af: Afrikaans dey mix Dutch precision with African rhythm, dey create staccato bursts perfect for house music drops. Jo'burg clubbers dey switch between Afrikaans, English, and Zulu mid-sentence without missing beat.
whyCity.af: Johannesburg club scene dey thrive for Newtown converted mine buildings and Braamfontein rooftops, where post-apartheid energy dey create dancefloors of pure transformation and healing through bass.
dialect.ga: Irish Gaelic ancient cadences dey flow through Dublin club scene like whispered secrets. The language poetic structure dey turn drunken shouts into inadvertent verse, make every night feel mythological.
whyCity.ga: Dublin nightlife dey span from Temple Bar tourist traps to underground venues for the Liberties, where traditional Irish hospitality meet modern hedonism. Every dancefloor become session.
dialect.kriol: Kriol dey blend Aboriginal languages with English for rapid-fire poetry wey sound like natural beatboxing. E be Australia most rhythmic language, born from cultural fusion and perfect for club chatter.
whyCity.kriol: Northern Australia festival circuit dey create temporary dancefloors under star-filled skies, where outback isolation dey breed intense community. Darwin tropical nights dey fuel non-stop outdoor parties with unmatched energy.
